title: Drop disable-model-invocation flag from activity recipe SKILL.mds + shell
description: The six activity recipe SKILL.mds (bugfix/feature/refactor/research/cleanup/docs-a-bead) and bead-lifecycle-shell carry 'disable-model-invocation: true' in frontmatter. This makes the Skill tool refuse to load them ('cannot be used with Skill tool due to disable-model-invocation'), which blocks the loom-1ab router from dispatching to recipes via Skill(<recipe>) — the canonical handoff path. The existing /bugfix-a-bead and /research-a-bead slash commands also say 'Invoke the X skill' and would hit the same wall (untested). FIX: remove 'disable-model-invocation: true' from frontmatter of all 7 SKILL.mds. Recipes are explicitly invoked via slash command (or via the router); the flag was belt-and-suspenders against accidental auto-fire. Descriptions are already narrow and workflow-mode off/light gating provides additional defense. ACCEPTANCE: 7 SKILL.mds edited; Skill(<any-recipe>) loads cleanly; existing /bugfix-a-bead and /research-a-bead slash commands work end-to-end. See loom/decisions drawer 0e40c06 (2026-05-03).
close_reason: Removed disable-model-invocation:true from 6 recipe SKILL.mds + bead-lifecycle-shell. Updated body text + manual.md. Unblocks loom-1ab router. commit c44d7cb.

title: loom: /wrap-up final step — deploy via install.sh when in loom checkout
description: When working on loom, the /wrap-up command should auto-detect the loom checkout (presence of install.sh + settings.snippet.json + skills/ + commands/ at pwd root) and run a deploy step as its final action. The deploy step needs to (a) mkdir any missing ~/.claude/skills/<new>/ parent dirs, (b) rm dangling symlinks owned by loom (target points into $LOOM_ROOT but file no longer exists), then (c) ./install.sh. This compensates for two install.sh gaps documented today in MemPalace drawer 'install.sh — TWO RENAME-DEPLOY GAPS FOUND' (loom/decisions, 2026-05-03). Until install.sh itself absorbs the fix (separate bead, P3), this addendum keeps loom self-deploying after every wrap-up. Edit only commands/wrap-up.md; the loom-detection check guards the step from firing in non-loom projects.
close_reason: Added section 6 to commands/wrap-up.md: detects loom checkout, mkdir's missing skill subdirs, prunes dangling loom-owned symlinks, runs ./install.sh. Section is no-op in non-loom projects. Cross-refs the install.sh gap drawer for lineage. Will collapse to plain ./install.sh once install.sh fix lands (separate bead).

title: v2: router — /working-a-bead becomes selector that dispatches by bead.type
description: commands/working-a-bead.md becomes a router. Logic: takes bead-id (optional --recipe=<name> override), runs bd show, scores against the 6 recipes by bead.type field + description heuristics. Routes to the matching recipe. On ambiguity (bead matches 2+ recipes plausibly): list ambiguous candidates with one-line 'because' for each and prompt user to pick or use --recipe=<name> override (Frank's locked decision 6, 2026-05-02). Default routing: type=bug → bugfix; type=feature → feature; type=epic → planning (TBD); type=task → check description keywords (refactor/cleanup/docs/research) with fallback to bugfix.

title: v2: research-a-bead recipe
description: skills/research-a-bead/SKILL.md. Activity-specific middle: define the question → search prior art (mempalace_search + kg_query + bd memories + diary_read) → fetch authoritative docs (context7 if library; web if needed) → synthesize → file findings as drawer + KG triples + optional follow-up beads. No code, no worktree (usually). Captures the discipline that was implicit in the deploy-day MemPalace search habit.
close_reason: research-a-bead recipe authored; sibling to bugfix-a-bead under epic loom-0y6

title: v2: feature-a-bead recipe
description: skills/feature-a-bead/SKILL.md. Activity-specific middle: brainstorm (superpowers/beadpowers) → writing-plans (multi-task) → TDD on NEW behavior (the contract) → integration test → bug-class equivalent for negative cases. Differs from bugfix: no symptom to reproduce; the RED test pins desired behavior, not failure. References the shared bead-lifecycle-shell for claim/isolate/verify/close/capture.
close_reason: recipe SKILL.md authored, install.sh symlinks deployed and verified

title: v2: refactor-a-bead recipe
description: skills/refactor-a-bead/SKILL.md. Activity-specific middle: identify scope → write characterization tests if missing (pin current behavior) → refactor → verify behavior preserved (all original tests pass) → close. Differs from bugfix: TDD inverted — tests come BEFORE the refactor and must KEEP passing. No new behavior; structural change only.
close_reason: recipe SKILL.md authored, install.sh symlinks deployed and verified

title: v2: rename working-a-bead → bugfix-a-bead + retitle for activity shape
description: Today working-a-bead is bug-shaped (forged during HAW deploy-day cleanup). Once the activity recipes land it should be one of N recipes the router selects among. Rename the skill directory + filename + frontmatter name, update internal references in router + session-startup hand-off + manual + walkthrough. Shell out the bug-specific middle (reproduce → RED → minimal GREEN → bug-class coverage → enshrined-test sweep) — the shared bead-lifecycle-shell skill already owns claim/isolate/verify/close/capture.
close_reason: Renamed skills/working-a-bead/ → skills/bugfix-a-bead/ + commands/working-a-bead.md → commands/bugfix-a-bead.md. SKILL.md rewritten as slim variable middle (M1-M5) with phases A/B/C/D delegated to bead-lifecycle-shell. All refs swept across install/uninstall/README/CLAUDE/manual/walkthrough/session-startup/shell/agents. workflow.json set to light. Commit bc841d7 merged to main as 47-line diff. Unblocks loom-1ab router and the 5 sibling recipes.

title: v2: activity-shaped recipes + router (continued from HAW bng)
description: Continuation of the v2 work originally tracked in HAW epic hundred-acre-woods-bng. The shared bead-lifecycle-shell skill (was 7o9) shipped before migration. Remaining: 5 activity recipes (feature, refactor, research, cleanup, docs) + the /working-a-bead router that selects among them by bead.type/heuristics + final pass on docs/manual.md and docs/walkthrough.md to reflect the multi-recipe shape. Design + locked decisions captured in MemPalace drawer 'RECIPE SHAPES — ACTIVITY MATRIX' (hundred_acre_woods/decisions, 2026-05-02) — see also LOOM PROVENANCE drawer in loom/decisions wing for the full inheritance chain. Frank's locked decisions: 6 recipes (bug already exists as bugfix-shaped working-a-bead; rename pending), shared shell as separate skill (shipped), router goes straight to selector pattern not phased intro, full schema for state file with stage as first-class.

title: v2: cleanup-a-bead recipe
description: skills/cleanup-a-bead/SKILL.md. Activity-specific middle: identify scope (often via bd orphans + bd stale + grep for stale references) → remove → verify nothing broke (full test suite + manual smoke if integration). No new tests typically. Distinct from refactor: removal vs restructure. Many follow-up beads from HAW deploy-day fit this shape (80v already shipped via trimmed working-a-bead; future cleanups should use this recipe).
close_reason: recipe SKILL.md authored, install.sh symlinks deployed and verified

title: v2: manual + walkthrough updates for multi-recipe shape
description: docs/manual.md section 4 changes from 'The 14-step bead recipe' (singular) to 'The recipes' (plural). New per-recipe sub-sections summarize each. Author docs/feature-walkthrough.md as companion to the existing bug-shaped walkthrough — narrative session walking through a real feature bead so the recipe shape is demonstrable. Also confirm wiki mirrors stay in sync (Frank handles wiki sync manually per 2026-05-03).

title: v2: docs-a-bead recipe
description: skills/docs-a-bead/SKILL.md. Activity-specific middle: identify gap (drift from code, missing context, stale section) → draft → review against code for accuracy → commit. No TDD beyond doctest/link-checking when applicable. The doc itself is the deliverable; capture is the doc, not a separate drawer (unless the doc captures a decision). Voice consistency matters.
close_reason: recipe SKILL.md authored, install.sh symlinks deployed and verified

title: git-push-bd-sync hook false-positive: tighten matcher when push target ≠ cwd .beads/
description: When pushing repo A from a cwd inside repo B with dirty .beads/, the git-push-bd-sync hook warns about B's beads even though the push is for A. Surfaced 2026-05-03 during loom extraction (multiple loom pushes triggered the warning because cwd was HAW with dirty .beads/). Fix: detect whether the dirty .beads/ belongs to the same repo as the push target. Only warn if the same. Use git rev-parse --show-toplevel + comparison with the remote URL git is pushing to. Document the new behavior in docs/manual.md hooks reference.
close_reason: Hook now resolves push target via cd-chain parsing; .beads/ presence and dirty-check run against target dir, not cwd. 11 fixture tests under lib/tests/. Docs updated. Light-mode close.

Inherited closed beads from HAW: epic hundred-acre-woods-2st (workflow-infra v1, 8/9 children closed: 3jn working-a-bead skill, 5a9 session-startup extensions, 24a slash commands, ivg subagents, 91d hooks, 7z5 path-scoped rules, qi0 settings/permissions, bhs CLAUDE.md updates; deferred: vjb agent teams pilot — stays in HAW as Phase-4-related). Plus epic hundred-acre-woods-bng (v2): 7o9 shared shell, ipj rename plan, jnd opt-out + state + statusline, bmi audit-project. The active v2 epic in loom (loom-?) supersedes bng's still-open children. Cross-reference: HAW migration marker hundred-acre-woods-1qd.
